item.title: Lockheed Martin cannot say when US allies will get Patriot missiles
item.description: Top executive says arms maker does not control allocation of PAC-3 interceptors, despite tripling production

item.title: FirstFT: Seven people charged over deadly Hong Kong fire
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: US launches fresh wave of strikes on Iran, and BoJ governor Kazuo Ueda admitted to hospital

item.title: UK to launch CO₂ supply push as pubs prepare for bumper beer sales
item.description: Consultation comes as football fans look forward to start of World Cup and month of drinking

item.title: US tech stocks sink as volatility flares up on Wall Street
item.description: Latest bout of selling comes ahead of SpaceX’s historic market debut

item.title: Oracle plans to raise $40bn for data centre build-out
item.description: The database group’s full-year capital expenditure rose to $55.7bn

item.title: EU to protect industries from future carbon costs if they invest in the bloc
item.description: Brussels weighs scheme to provide free emission allowances beyond the existing 2039 schedule

item.title: Oil tankers increase ‘dark’ transits through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Donald Trump says US launched ‘secret mission’ to help move crude through the waterway

item.title: Historic Merseyside shipyard bought ahead of planned Royal Navy refresh
item.description: Cammell Laird, once the engine room of warship building in the UK, positions for potential higher defence spending

item.title: Mike Ashley’s Frasers offers to buy Hugo Boss in €2.7bn deal
item.description: Retail billionaire could use bid to increase stake in German fashion group rather than taking full control

item.title: Pay trends suggest US bosses are wealthier and more nervous
item.description: More than 29% of S&P 500 companies provided home security perks for their top brass in 2025

item.title: Trump’s AI fund idea is good politics, but bad economics
item.description: Plans to share the gains from technology could cause more problems than they solve

item.title: Apollo and Blackstone raise $35bn in chip financing deal for Anthropic
item.description: Transaction is one of the largest private credit fundraisings, fuelling Claude maker’s AI growth plans

item.title: AI and the productivity paradox
item.description: Routine time savings don’t automatically make organisations function better — and staff have to clean up a lot of slop

item.title: Trump baffles Wall Street with top dealmaker praise for Citi
item.description: A morning social media post from the president congratulates bank on a ranking it does not hold

item.title: Here’s that ‘SpaceX’s IPO is overvalued by 114%’ research in full
item.description: Notes on a panhandle

item.title: German start-up Neura raises $1.4bn in humanoid robot push
item.description: Crypto group Tether, Amazon and Nvidia invest in fundraising deal that values company at about $7bn

item.title: Apollo executive warns private equity has ‘price to pay’ for boom years
item.description: Scott Kleinman says investors have decided the market got ‘a little out of whack’

item.title: Companies would like you to know they are doing things on purpose
item.description: The resistible rise of ‘with intention’

item.title: Changes to small UK company filing rules prompt backlash
item.description: Previous business secretary vowed to drop the plans to avoid imposing regulation on entrepreneurs

item.title: Octopus Energy tells regulator it has reached capital buffer target
item.description: Rivals had hit out at UK’s largest household supplier, arguing it was unfair it could keep taking on customers

item.title: What happened to the great rebirth of American manufacturing?
item.description: Skills shortages, shifting tariffs and complex permitting processes are major barriers

item.title: Climate reporting is still a thing for US companies
item.description: Donald Trump may have dropped Biden-era rules but state law requires sustainability reporting

item.title: How tin went from baked beans to AI gravy train
item.description: Higher prices are prompting some miners to take up their shovels

item.title: Germany seeks to lead fighter jet project after ditching France
item.description: Berlin vows to look for other partners in return for role corresponding to its ‘financial contribution’

item.title: FirstFT: US and Iran trade fire after downing of American helicopter
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: IPO boom threatens US equity supplies and newcomer wins Maine primary

item.title: Barry Diller says MGM’s physical assets are more valuable in AI age
item.description: Chair of People Inc views $18bn takeover bid for casino group as hedge against magazine business

item.title: Workspace to raise rents as it seeks to fend off Boaz Weinstein
item.description: Plan to boost profits comes as flexible office landlord reported a £120mn loss on falling property values and rents

item.title: Anthropic leans into AI’s nascent slice-and-dice era
item.description: Clever financial engineering is allowing conservative, risk-averse investors to participate enthusiastically

item.title: Ares raises $8.5bn for newest fund in spite of private markets woes
item.description: Commitments underscore demand for debt backed by data centres, railcars, music royalties and car leases

item.title: WHSmith shares tumble 17% as retailer announces capital raise
item.description: London-listed company warns of hit to profits from the Middle East conflict
